title: Troubleshooting Production Failures
concept: Troubleshooting production vision models requires analyzing telemetry, identifying environmental glitches, and performing rollbacks.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is uncertainty sampling in active learning?

options.text: Flagging images where the model's prediction confidence is low or near the decision boundary
Adding random Gaussian noise to the input images
Selecting only the highest resolution files for training

exercises.explain: Uncertainty sampling targets files that fall near decision boundaries where the model lacks clear confidence.

cards.title: When AI fails in the wild
cards.body: It's 2 AM, and you get an alert: the factory inspection AI is suddenly letting defective items pass. What's wrong? Is it a software bug, a model update, or did a worker bump the camera? Diagnosing wild failures is the ultimate skill of an ML engineer!

cards.title: Failure Diagnostics in Production
cards.body: Production troubleshooting involves: Telemetry Analysis (checking CPU/GPU metrics and error logs), Input Auditing (checking if cameras are out-of-focus or dirty), and Rollbacks (instantly reverting to a stable model checkpoint).

cards.title: A flight data recorder
cards.body: When a plane has a rough landing, investigators read the "black box" flight recorder to find out what happened. Production logging is your black box: it stores input-output metadata so you can replay the exact frames that caused a failure.

cards.title: The vibrating bracket bug
cards.body: A street camera starts outputting gibberish. The engineer checks the raw input logs and notices the images are blurred. They visit the site and discover a loose mounting bracket causing the camera to vibrate in the wind. The model was fine; the hardware was broken!

cards.title: Not always a code problem
cards.body: You might think that when AI fails, you should immediately rewrite the code or change hyperparameters. In reality, real-world vision failures are frequently physical: lens glare, dust, vibrations, or bad cable connections that distort image files.

cards.title: Production Troubleshooting Flow
cards.body: Steps to resolve a sudden vision failure:

left.text:
Check error telemetry logs
Roll back to stable model v1.0
Restore system functionality
Stop the bleeding

right.text:
Inspect raw input frame logs
Check camera focus & alignment
Test file headers & sizes
Patch physical/digital bug

exercises.prompt: What is the first step you should take if a newly deployed model v2.0 causes critical errors in a live production environment?

options.text: Roll back the system to the last stable model version (v1.0)
Immediately start retraining v2.0 on a larger dataset
Re-export the model graph to ONNX format

exercises.explain: Reverting to a known stable model (rollback) immediately restores operations while you investigate the bug.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Physical hardware issues, like a dirty camera lens or loose mount, are common sources of AI failures in production.
exercises.explain: True. If the physical camera inputs degrade, the model receives out-of-distribution frames and performs poorly.

exercises.sentence: Reverting a production system to a previous stable software version is a ___.
exercises.explain: A rollback resets the active application state to a known healthy backup checkpoint.

exercises.prompt: Which tool is most valuable for diagnosing why a model misclassified a specific image yesterday?

options.text: Saved input-output telemetry metadata and logs

exercises.explain: Logging input images and output confidence ratings allows engineers to replay and analyze failures post-incident.

exercises.prompt: A conveyor-belt model starts missing defect classifications after a shift change. You check server logs and find no software errors. The GPU runs normally. What should you do next?

options.text: Inspect the raw video stream logs to check if the camera was bumped, out of focus, or if lighting was turned off during the shift change.
Delete the model database and rewrite the convolutional layers.
Increase the learning rate and restart training from scratch.
Convert the model to INT8 precision.

exercises.explain: Physical changes in the production environment (alignment, focus, lighting shifts) are the most common source of sudden, silent vision model failures.
